narrative: OMX30 enters Tuesday on a constructive global backdrop with European indices firm (DAX +0.28%, CAC +0.69%) and Asian bourses rallying hard (Nikkei +2.17%). The index remains structurally bullish at +6.8% above its 200-day moving average, though technicals show mixed conviction: RSI sits neutral at 51.07, MACD is below signal (hist=-4.283), and ADX of 10.4 signals weak momentum with a bearish directional bias. Commodity moves favour selective exposure—copper +1.41% and gold +0.53% benefit Boliden, while oil's -2.24% slide eases industrial input costs. The key friction point is weak USD/SEK (-0.36%), which pressures large exporters, though the 35bp rise in US 10Y yields supports bank net interest margins. VIX compression to 18.19 suggests appetite for risk, but combined with the low ADX reading, we recommend a cautious bullish tilt with reduced conviction.
morningBrief: OMX30 set for modest gains (+0.3% to +0.5%) on positive global correlation and risk-on backdrop, but expect choppy, range-bound tape given weak technicals (ADX 10.4, MACD below signal). Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) are the brightest spot—US 10Y at 4.552% widens NIM tailwinds—but watch the 50-MA at 3180 as upside resistance; support is solid at lower Bollinger (circa 3085). Exporters (Atlas Copco, Volvo B, Ericsson B) face headwinds from weak USD/SEK, though Boliden warrants a long watch on copper strength. Oil weakness is a modest tailwind for consumer and industrials. Key level: yesterday's close ~3123; hold above for continuation bias. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is the only data event; watch for any surprise. Keep stops tight—ADX below 18 means trend conviction is low and whipsaws are elevated.
drivers: Positive global correlation signal (+0.408%) with strength in DAX/CAC and Asia (Nikkei +2.17%, Shanghai +1.28%)
OMX30 structurally bullish: +6.8% above 200-MA, in bull market regime despite weak ADX (10.4)
Copper +1.41% and gold +0.53% support mining/industrials; weak oil (-2.24%) reduces input cost drag
USD/SEK weakness (-0.36%) headwind for exporters partially offset by rising US 10Y (+35bp) supporting banks
VIX compression (-3.86%) indicates risk-on sentiment, though RSI 51.07 and Stochastic neutrality suggest limited upside momentum

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield jumped +35bp to 4.552%, widening NIM expectations for Swedish systemically important banks. Rates stay in supportive territory.
Copper +1.41% and gold +0.53% provide direct upside to mining revenues; Boliden is the primary beneficiary in OMX30.
Weak USD/SEK (-0.36%) pressures export pricing, though lower oil (-2.24%) partially offsets input cost relief. Net effect near flat.
NASDAQ +0.86% supports sentiment, but weak USD/SEK and low ADX momentum limit upside. Maintain hold bias.
Rising US 10Y yields (+35bp) increase long-duration refinancing costs and compress property valuations via DCF models.
Oil weakness (-2.24%) eases discretionary spending pressure, but weak EUR/SEK impacts sourcing costs marginally. Offsetting forces.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.41% and gold +0.53% provide strong direct revenue tailwinds. Entry on any dip to moving averages.
US 10Y +35bp widens Swedish bank NIM; trading near technical support with yield support intact.
Weak USD/SEK and low ADX momentum create headwinds despite NASDAQ strength. Await clearer trend definition.
Export-sensitive; USD/SEK weakness is a headwind. Monitor oil price for input cost relief offset.
Bullish structure (+6.8% above 200-MA) but weak momentum (ADX 10.4, MACD below signal). Expect 3085–3180 range trade.

econCalendarAlert: No Swedish economic releases scheduled. US Jobless Claims (expected 220k, prior 231k) at 14:30 CET—key risk event for USD/SEK and global risk sentiment.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (Trump–Netanyahu tensions noted in headlines) or surprise weak US labour data could spike VIX, break risk-on sentiment, and trigger reversal toward support. ADX weakness (10.4) means any selling can accelerate quickly in a low-conviction market.
technicalSummary: +0.8% above 50-MA (3123 pivot), RSI 51.07 neutral—no overbought/oversold flag
MACD below signal (hist=-4.283) suggests momentum loss; Stochastic K=28.5 D=36.9 near neutral
ADX 10.4 (below 18 threshold): trend conviction LOW, reduce confidence 30% and expect chop
Bollinger %B=0.47 in lower half; width 5.58% suggests room for expansion
52-week positioning 85% of range, -3.6% from high—room to run but no breakout conviction yet
